commandsRun.observation: Installed the Chromium bundle required for agent-browser screenshots on this machine.

Sequentially queried GET https://blockscout-testnet.polkadot.io/api/v2/smart-contracts/<address> for all 12 canonical addresses; 8 addresses returned is_verified=true, 3 returned is_verified=false with verified_twin_address_hash, and quoteEngine returned no source_code or verification fields.

Opened each canonical address on Blockscout Contract tab and saved screenshot/text captures for all 12 contracts. The UI showed exact-match verification for 8 contracts, “Contract is not verified” banners for AccessManager/WPAS/USDCMock, and only creation code + “Verify & publish” for quoteEngine.

commandsRun.command: agent-browser --session explorercheck errors && agent-browser --session explorercheck console | tail -n 50
commandsRun.observation: Browser console recorded Blockscout 429 responses, client-side exceptions, and reCAPTCHA site-key errors during batched browsing.

Compared /home/kpa/polkadot/dualvm/deployments/polkadot-hub-testnet-canonical-verification.json against live Blockscout evidence and found contradictions for DualVMAccessManager, WPAS, and USDCMock: the artifact says verified, but Blockscout still marks the deployed addresses unverified.
Confirmed the frontend reads the 12 canonical manifest addresses, but 4 of those addresses are not explorer-verified on Blockscout.

assertions.title: All contracts explorer-verified on Blockscout

steps.action: Load the canonical deployment manifest and enumerate every contract address.
steps.expected: 12 canonical contract addresses to inspect on Blockscout.

steps.action: Query Blockscout smart-contract API for each canonical address.
steps.expected: Every address should return verified source status on the deployed address itself.
steps.observed: Only 8/12 addresses returned is_verified=true. AccessManager, WPAS, and USDCMock returned is_verified=false with verified_twin_address_hash, while quoteEngine returned no source_code/is_verified fields at all.

steps.action: Inspect the Blockscout Contract tab for the canonical addresses.
steps.expected: Each page should show a verified-source banner/source tab for the deployed address.
steps.observed: Oracle/RiskAdapter/MarketVersionRegistry/GovernanceToken/DualVMGovernor/TimelockController/DebtPool/LendingCore showed “Contract Source Code Verified (Exact Match)”. AccessManager/WPAS/USDCMock showed a yellow “Contract is not verified” banner despite displaying twin-bytecode source, and quoteEngine showed only creation code plus “Verify & publish”.

steps.action: Compare the committed verification artifact to live Blockscout evidence.
steps.expected: Artifact and explorer should agree that every canonical address is verified.
steps.observed: The artifact contradicts live Blockscout for AccessManager, WPAS, and USDCMock: it records status="verified" for those addresses, but Blockscout still marks the deployed addresses unverified.

evidence.consoleErrors: Blockscout emitted 429 rate-limit errors, Next.js client-side exceptions, and reCAPTCHA site-key errors during batch page inspection; evidence was still collected via sequential retries and saved page captures.
evidence.network: Sequential GET /api/v2/smart-contracts/<address> -> 200 for all 12 canonical addresses. Exact-match verified on 8 addresses; AccessManager/WPAS/USDCMock returned is_verified=false with verified_twin_address_hash; quoteEngine returned only creation/deployed bytecode fields. See consolidated-deployment/explorer-checks/blockscout-smart-contract-v2-summary.json and consolidated-deployment/explorer-checks/verification-artifact-vs-explorer.json.

assertions.issues: VAL-DEPLOY-001 fails because not every canonical manifest contract is explorer-verified on the deployed address. Live Blockscout marks AccessManager, WPAS, and USDCMock as unverified twin-bytecode matches, and quoteEngine is not verified at all.

assertions.title: Explorer-verified contracts match frontend reads

steps.action: Inspect src/lib/manifest.ts to identify what deployment manifest the frontend reads.
steps.expected: Frontend should resolve to the canonical deployment manifest used for explorer checks.
steps.observed: src/lib/manifest.ts imports ../../deployments/polkadot-hub-testnet-canonical.json directly and exports its parsed contents.

steps.action: Compare frontend-read addresses with the canonical manifest addresses.
steps.expected: Frontend-read addresses should equal the canonical manifest addresses.
steps.observed: All 12 frontend-read addresses match the canonical manifest exactly.

steps.action: Compare those frontend-read addresses with live Blockscout verification state.
steps.expected: Every frontend-read address should correspond to an explorer-verified deployed address.
steps.observed: Four frontend-read addresses are not explorer-verified on Blockscout: accessManager (0x32d0...c2f0), wpas (0x9b9e...10F1), usdc (0x75d4...Ef06), and quoteEngine (0x9a78...76DE).

evidence.consoleErrors: Same Blockscout 429/client-side exception/reCAPTCHA errors as above during hosted page browsing; the manifest comparison itself was file-based and unaffected.
evidence.network: Frontend manifest import resolved to deployments/polkadot-hub-testnet-canonical.json. Comparing those addresses against GET /api/v2/smart-contracts/<address> shows only 8/12 frontend-read addresses are explorer-verified; see consolidated-deployment/explorer-checks/frontend-manifest-import-summary.json and consolidated-deployment/explorer-checks/frontend-vs-explorer-summary.json.

assertions.issues: VAL-CROSS-004 fails because the frontend reads the canonical manifest addresses exactly, but 4 of those frontend-read addresses are not explorer-verified on Blockscout. Address alignment exists; verification alignment does not.

frictions.description: Blockscout rate-limited batched browsing/API access with repeated HTTP 429 responses, client-side exceptions, and reCAPTCHA-site-key errors.
frictions.resolution: Retried sequentially with slower pacing and recorded both browser captures and eventual /api/v2/smart-contracts responses.

frictions.description: For some deployed addresses, Blockscout shows source code from a same-bytecode twin while simultaneously labeling the current address as unverified, which can look superficially verified if only the code pane is checked.
frictions.resolution: Used the yellow “Contract is not verified” banner plus /api/v2/smart-contracts.is_verified and verified_twin_address_hash fields to determine the deployed-address status.
